criteria.label: Explain the role of train, validation, and test metrics in one experiment.
criteria.example: Write three bullets: train/* monitors learning on fit data; val/* drives selection each epoch; test/* is logged once at final sign-off.

criteria.label: Name metrics using split prefixes for a classification run.
criteria.example: List train/loss, train/accuracy, val/loss, val/f1, and test/f1 as the keys you would log— with val/f1 as the primary selection metric.

criteria.label: Interpret diverging train and validation curves.
criteria.example: Given train/loss falling and val/loss rising after epoch 15, state overfitting and recommend early stopping at the best val/loss epoch.

criteria.label: Implement a loop that logs train and val F1 with consistent keys.
criteria.example: Show a for-loop over hyperparameters that appends dicts with keys train/f1 and val/f1, then picks best by max val/f1.

criteria.label: Design a logging policy that keeps test metrics locked.
criteria.example: Describe train.py logging train/* and val/* only; release.py loading the best val checkpoint and logging test/f1 once with phase=final_report.
